metadata.title: GovForge — Governance infrastructure for AI coding agents
metadata.description: Audit, review and govern every code decision produced by Claude Code, Codex, Cursor and other AI coding agents. Local-first, Git-native, Apache 2.0.
metadata.ogTitle: GovForge — Governance infrastructure for AI coding agents
metadata.ogDescription: Audit, review and govern every code decision produced by AI coding agents. Local-first, Git-native, Apache 2.0.
metadata.twitterTitle: GovForge — Governance infrastructure for AI coding agents
metadata.twitterDescription: Audit, review and govern every code decision produced by AI coding agents.

footer.tagline: Governance infrastructure for AI coding agents.

hero.subtitle: Audit, review and control every code decision produced by Claude Code, Codex, Cursor and other AI coding agents.

supportedAgents.heading: Works with the agents you already use

problemStatement.title: AI agents now write production code.
problemStatement.subtitle: Most teams have no idea what they decided, or why.

features.heading: What GovForge gives you
features.subheading: Not another agent. The infrastructure to govern the ones you already use.

cards.title: Decision Records
cards.description: Every code change becomes a structured decision: author, intent, rationale, risk, status.

cards.title: Policy Engine
cards.description: Block changes that touch auth, secrets, or schema without explicit review and approval.

cards.title: Audit Timeline
cards.description: Append-only event log linked to every commit, review, finding, and approval.

cards.title: Structured Disagreement
cards.description: Capture conflicts between agents as first-class artifacts — not buried in chat history.

cards.title: Human Approval
cards.description: High-risk diffs require a human signature. With full context, not blind rubber-stamping.

cards.title: Git-aware Reviews
cards.description: Reviews from another agent attached to lines, files, and commits. Findings, not opinions.

workflow.heading: How it works
workflow.subheading: Four steps. Real CLI commands. Real audit trail at the end.

architecture.heading: Local-first. Git-native.
architecture.subheading: Everything runs on your machine. No cloud unless you choose. Optional team sync (Phase 3) for collaboration and enterprise.
architecture.footnote: The MCP server is the integration point. Everything else is offline-by-default.

ossVsEnterprise.heading: Open core. Pay only for what teams actually need.

docs.metaDescription: Architecture, data model, MCP integration, threat model, workflow walkthrough — every doc lives in the GitHub repo.
docs.heading: Documentation
docs.subheading: Until the dedicated docs site lands (Phase 2), every document lives in the GitHub repository under docs/. Each card links straight to the canonical source.

cards.title: Architecture
cards.desc: Components, layers, sequence diagram, audit-log invariant.

cards.title: Data model
cards.desc: 12 entities, ER diagram, decision-status state machine, enum reference.

cards.title: MCP integration
cards.desc: Wiring guides for Claude Code, Codex, Cursor, Cline. Tool reference.

cards.title: Threat model
cards.desc: Security guarantees and the tests that pin each one in CI.

cards.title: Workflow example
cards.desc: 13-step Claude → Codex → human-approval walkthrough with real CLI output.

cards.title: Brand guide
cards.desc: Wordmark, palette, typography, tagline, tone of voice.

cards.title: Changelog
cards.desc: Keep-a-Changelog format. [Unreleased] groups every Phase 1 workstream.

cards.title: Contributing
cards.desc: Local setup per package, commit convention, scope-specific recipes.

install.heading: Install in 30 seconds.
install.subheading: Pick a channel. Source works today; the others come with the first tagged release.

tabs.label: Source
tabs.caption: clone + build
tabs.command:
git clone https://github.com/ericvaillancourt/govforge && cd govforge
cd cli && go build -o ~/bin/gf ./cmd/gf
cd ../backend && pip install -e .
tabs.note: Requires Go 1.22+ and Python 3.12+. Pure-Go SQLite driver — no cgo needed.

tabs.label: Homebrew
tabs.caption: macOS / Linux
tabs.note: Tap moves to govforge/homebrew-tap once the GitHub org transfer lands.

tabs.label: pipx
tabs.caption: any OS with Python 3.12+
tabs.note: Installs the backend (services, MCP server, HTTP API). The gf CLI is a separate Go binary.

tabs.label: Pre-built binary
tabs.caption: linux / darwin / windows × amd64 / arm64
tabs.note: Detects your OS + arch and downloads the matching gf binary from GitHub Releases.

compliance.heading: Built for code that actually has consequences.
compliance.subheading: GovForge runs entirely on your machine. The threat model is pinned by tests in CI — every guarantee is grep-able.

guarantees.title: Local-first by default
guarantees.desc: All data lives in .govforge/govforge.db. Backend binds to 127.0.0.1; MCP transport is stdio. No outbound network calls in Phase 1.

guarantees.title: Read-only Git
guarantees.desc: The extractor uses an allowlist of seven verbs (diff/show/log/rev_parse/ls_tree/rev_list/cat_file). A regression that adds push/reset/checkout/commit fails CI.

guarantees.title: No shell, no eval
guarantees.desc: MCP tools never spawn a subprocess and never call eval/exec. The MCP package is grep-asserted on every PR.

guarantees.title: Append-only audit log
guarantees.desc: Every mutating service writes an Event row. The timeline can be replayed from the events table alone.

guarantees.title: Apache 2.0
guarantees.desc: Open source, enterprise-friendly. No CLA. Auditors can read every line; legal teams can ship without sign-off.

guarantees.title: Compliance-ready foundations
guarantees.desc: Local audit log + signed approvals + per-decision risk classification map onto SOC 2, Loi 25 (Quebec), and the EU AI Act. Phase 3 adds the formal reporting layer.

trustStrip.heading: Built for code that actually matters.

items.title: Local-first
items.desc: Your code never leaves your machine.

finalCta.titleLine1: Stop trusting AI agents on faith.

pricing.metaDescription: Open source forever. Pay only when teams need cloud sync, RBAC, SSO, or compliance reporting.
pricing.heading: Pricing as boring as governance should be.
pricing.subheading: Open core. Free OSS forever. Pay only when teams need collaboration or compliance.
pricing.noLockIn: No lock-in. Switch tiers anytime. OSS works forever even if SaaS shuts down.

tiers.name: Open Source
tiers.description: Everything you need to govern AI agents on your own machine.

tiers.name: Team
tiers.description: Cloud sync, shared timelines, notifications. (Coming Phase 3.)

tiers.name: Enterprise
tiers.description: Air-gapped, RBAC, SSO/SAML, compliance reports, SLA.

faq.q: Is the OSS version really fully functional?
faq.a: Yes. Phase 1 ships a complete local-first product. You can govern AI agents end-to-end without paying us a cent.

faq.q: Can I self-host the Enterprise features?
faq.a: Yes — air-gapped deployment is one of the core Enterprise features. Cloud sync becomes optional rather than mandatory.

faq.q: Do you store any of my code?
faq.a: Never on the OSS tier. On Team/Enterprise, only the metadata you explicitly choose to sync (decisions, reviews, events) — never raw source code.

faq.q: When will Team / Enterprise be available?
faq.a: Team: Phase 3 (post-launch). Enterprise: pilot programs available now via direct contact, full GA after Team.

faq.q: What if you shut down the SaaS one day?
faq.a: The OSS core works forever, fully self-hosted, with no dependency on our cloud. There is no vendor lock-in by design.
